parityMeta.describes: PA-S0 i18n/terms data port — a full snapshot of the eleven frozen label tables (TERMS / PHASE_LABELS / EFFECTIVE_PHASE_DISPLAY_LABELS / INTENSITY_BIAS_LABELS / TECHNIQUE_QUALITY_LABELS / SUPPORT_BLOCK_LABELS / SKIP_REASON_LABELS / DELOAD_LEVEL_LABELS / DELOAD_STRATEGY_LABELS / READINESS_ADJUSTMENT_LABELS / MUSCLE_LABELS from src/i18n/terms.ts) keyed by their TS keys, plus per-table counts and every TERMS key routed through term(). The Swift port (IronPathL10n.Terms) reconstructs the same snapshot from its mirrored tables + term() and asserts it equals this golden entry-by-entry (Chinese values verbatim), so no label can be dropped or altered in transcription.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: No input params — the generator transcribes the static, committed label tables directly from src/i18n/terms.ts (no engine call, no clock, generatedAtPolicy 'none'). terms.ts is the PA track's one clean leaf (zero imports, zero runtime logic). De-dup note: terms.MUSCLE_LABELS (5: chest/back/legs/shoulders/arms -> 胸/背/腿/肩/手臂) is a DISTINCT TS constant from formatters.ts MUSCLE_LABELS (18 entries, already ported into IronPathTrainingDecision.VolumeAdaptationEngine.muscleLabels); the two share these 5 Chinese values but have different key universes and live in different source files — not merged here. The S4 i18n/formatters port consolidates the shared muscle vocabulary.
